name: ask-break-it-down
title: Question Scaffold
description: Break one big question into a prioritized question stack using first principles and explicit data/control framing. Use when the scope is too broad or multiple goals are tangled together.
purpose: Turn one broad problem statement into a small, prioritized stack of askable subquestions.
when_to_use: Need to split one large question into smaller askable units.
Need to separate business, technical, and user layers instead of mixing them.
Need a first-principles and data/control view before asking the final question.
do_not_use: Need to clarify the raw problem first because the topic is still foggy.
Need a final answer rather than question design.

required_inputs.name: AUDIENCE
required_inputs.description: Who the future answer is meant to help.

required_inputs.name: CONSTRAINTS
required_inputs.description: Known business, technical, or evidence constraints.

input_contract_notes: PROBLEM_STATEMENT should contain one bounded direction, not a bundle of unrelated asks.
If multiple purposes are mixed together, separate them into business, technical, and user-experience layers first.
Use first principles only to remove confusion, not to expand into theoretical essay writing.

structured_outputs.name: FIRST_PRINCIPLES
structured_outputs.description: Domain principles that should stay stable while the question is reframed.

structured_outputs.name: DATA_CONTROL_VIEW
structured_outputs.description: Data-first and explicit-control framing of the question.

structured_outputs.name: CORE_QUESTION
structured_outputs.description: Single main question to ask first.

output_contract_notes: QUESTION_LAYERS should contain only 3 to 5 questions in total.
CORE_QUESTION should be the smallest question that unlocks the rest of the stack.
DATA_CONTROL_VIEW should make the flow or control boundary more concrete, not more abstract.
neutrality_rules: Do not answer the question while scaffolding it.
Prefer one prioritized stack over many equal-weight branches.
If a layer does not materially change the decision, omit it.
execution_constraints: Separate business purpose, technical implementation, and user experience when the prompt mixes them.
Use Hickey-Carmack style data-first and explicit-control framing to remove hidden complexity.
Keep the stack minimal and focused on the core question.
default_program: [stages: preflight>detect>analyze>plan>handoff>audit | scope: repo|diff|paths(glob,...) | policy: evidence,quality-gates{docs},deterministic-output | lens: hickey-carmack | output: md(contract=v1)]
lens_rationale: Question scaffolding should expose the core invariant, split layers cleanly, and choose the smallest explicit question stack that preserves the real decision.
